```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts

assert target_probability > 0.8, f"Target states probability {target_probability:.3f} should be > 0.8 after diffusion"

non_target_states = set(counts.keys()) - target_states
if non_target_states:
    max_non_target_count = max(counts.get(state, 0) for state in non_target_states)
    min_target_count = min(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
    assert min_target_count > max_non_target_count, f"Each target state count should exceed any non-target state count"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts

assert target_probability > 0.7, f"Target states probability {target_probability:.3f} should be > 0.7 after diffusion"
assert all(counts.get(state, 0) > 0.1 * shots for state in target_states), "Both target states should have significant counts"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts if total_counts > 0 else 0

assert all(counts.get(state, 0) / total_counts > 0.1 for state in target_states), "Both target states should have significant probability"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts

assert target_probability > 0.8, f"Expected high probability on target states, got {target_probability:.3f}"

for state in target_states:
    state_probability = counts.get(state, 0) / total_counts
    assert state_probability > 0.3, f"Target state {state} has too low probability: {state_probability:.3f}"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts

assert target_probability > 0.7, f"Target states should be amplified but only got {target_probability:.3f} probability"

non_target_states = set(counts.keys()) - target_states
if non_target_states:
    max_non_target_count = max(counts.get(state, 0) for state in non_target_states)
    min_target_count = min(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
    assert min_target_count > max_non_target_count, "Each target state should have more counts than any non-target state"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_probability = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states) / shots
non_target_probability = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in counts if state not in target_states) / shots

assert target_probability > 0.7, f"Target states probability {target_probability:.3f} should be > 0.7"
assert target_probability > non_target_probability, f"Target probability {target_probability:.3f} should exceed non-target probability {non_target_probability:.3f}"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts

assert target_probability > 0.7, f"Target states probability {target_probability:.3f} should be > 0.7 after diffusion"

for state in target_states:
    state_prob = counts.get(state, 0) / total_counts
    assert state_prob > 0.2, f"Target state {state} probability {state_prob:.3f} should be > 0.2"

non_target_probability = 1 - target_probability
assert non_target_probability < 0.3, f"Non-target states probability {non_target_probability:.3f} should be < 0.3"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts if total_counts > 0 else 0

# After Grover's algorithm with diffusion, the marked states should have high probability
assert target_probability > 0.7, f"Target states probability {target_probability:.3f} should be > 0.7"

# Each target state should have significant probability
for state in target_states:
    state_prob = counts.get(state, 0) / total_counts if total_counts > 0 else 0
    assert state_prob > 0.2, f"Target state {state} probability {state_prob:.3f} should be > 0.2"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts if total_counts > 0 else 0

assert target_probability > 0.5, f"Target states probability {target_probability} should be > 0.5 after diffusion"
assert all(counts.get(state, 0) > counts.get(non_target, 0) for state in target_states for non_target in ["000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "111"] if non_target not in target_states), "Each target state should have higher counts than non-target states"
```

```python
target_states = {"101", "110"}
```
```python
target_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in target_states)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
target_probability = target_counts / total_counts if total_counts > 0 else 0

assert target_probability > 0.5, f"Target states should be amplified, got probability {target_probability}"
assert target_counts > 0, "At least one target state should be measured"

for state in target_states:
    state_prob = counts.get(state, 0) / total_counts if total_counts > 0 else 0
    assert state_prob >= 0, f"State {state} probability should be non-negative"
```
